verses.esv: Long ago, at many times and in many ways, God spoke to our fathers by the prophets,
verses.kjv: GOD, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
verses.bsb: On many past occasions and in many different ways, God spoke to our fathers through the prophets.

verses.esv: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed the heir of all things, through whom also he created the world.
verses.kjv: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
verses.bsb: But in these last days He has spoken to us by His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, and through whom He made the universe.

verses.esv: He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature, and he upholds the universe by the word of his power. After making purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
verses.kjv: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
verses.bsb: The Son is the radiance of God’s glory and the exact representation of His nature, upholding all things by His powerful word. After He had provided purification for sins, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.

verses.esv: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
verses.kjv: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
verses.bsb: So He became as far superior to the angels as the name He has inherited is excellent beyond theirs.

verses.esv: For to which of the angels did God ever say, “You are my Son, today I have begotten you”? Or again, “I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son”?
verses.kjv: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?
verses.bsb: For to which of the angels did God ever say:  “You are My Son; today I have become Your Father”?   Or again:  “I will be His Father, and He will be My Son”?

verses.esv: And again, when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, “Let all God's angels worship him.”
verses.kjv: And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.
verses.bsb: And again, when God brings His firstborn into the world, He says:  “Let all God’s angels worship Him.”

verses.esv: Of the angels he says, “He makes his angels winds, and his ministers a flame of fire.”
verses.kjv: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.
verses.bsb: Now about the angels He says:  “He makes His angels winds, His servants flames of fire.”

verses.esv: But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom.
verses.kjv: But unto the Son he saith, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.
verses.bsb: But about the Son He says:  “Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever, and justice is the scepter of Your kingdom.

verses.esv: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.”
verses.kjv: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
verses.bsb: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, Your God, has anointed You above Your companions with the oil of joy.”

verses.esv: And, “You, Lord, laid the foundation of the earth in the beginning, and the heavens are the work of your hands;
verses.kjv: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:
verses.bsb: And:  “In the beginning, O Lord, You laid the foundations of the earth, and the heavens are the work of Your hands.

verses.esv: they will perish, but you remain; they will all wear out like a garment,
verses.kjv: They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
verses.bsb: They will perish, but You remain; they will all wear out like a garment.

verses.esv: like a robe you will roll them up, like a garment they will be changed. But you are the same, and your years will have no end.”
verses.kjv: And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
verses.bsb: You will roll them up like a robe; like a garment they will be changed; but You remain the same, and Your years will never end.”

verses.esv: And to which of the angels has he ever said, “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet”?
verses.kjv: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?
verses.bsb: Yet to which of the angels did God ever say:  “Sit at My right hand until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet”?

verses.esv: Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?
verses.kjv: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?
verses.bsb: Are not the angels ministering spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation?

verses.esv: Therefore we must pay much closer attention to what we have heard, lest we drift away from it.
verses.kjv: THEREFORE we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let them slip.
verses.bsb: We must pay closer attention, therefore, to what we have heard, so that we do not drift away.

verses.esv: For since the message declared by angels proved to be reliable, and every transgression or disobedience received a just retribution,
verses.kjv: For if the word spoken by angels was stedfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompence of reward;
verses.bsb: For if the message spoken by angels was binding, and every transgression and disobedience received its just punishment,

verses.esv: how shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation? It was declared at first by the Lord, and it was attested to us by those who heard,
verses.kjv: How shall we escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard him;
verses.bsb: how shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation?  This salvation was first announced by the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard Him,

verses.esv: while God also bore witness by signs and wonders and various miracles and by gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to his will.
verses.kjv: God also bearing them witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?
verses.bsb: and was affirmed by God through signs, wonders, various miracles, and gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to His will.

verses.esv: For it was not to angels that God subjected the world to come, of which we are speaking.
verses.kjv: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.
verses.bsb: For it is not to angels that He has subjected the world to come, about which we are speaking.

verses.esv: It has been testified somewhere, “What is man, that you are mindful of him, or the son of man, that you care for him?
verses.kjv: But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
verses.bsb: But somewhere it is testified in these words:  “What is man that You are mindful of him, or the son of man that You care for him?

verses.esv: You made him for a little while lower than the angels; you have crowned him with glory and honor,
verses.kjv: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:
verses.bsb: You made him a little lower than the angels; You crowned him with glory and honor

verses.esv: putting everything in subjection under his feet.” Now in putting everything in subjection to him, he left nothing outside his control. At present, we do not yet see everything in subjection to him.
verses.kjv: Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing that is not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.
verses.bsb: and placed everything under his feet.”   When God subjected all things to him, He left nothing outside of his control. Yet at present we do not see everything subject to him.

verses.esv: But we see him who for a little while was made lower than the angels, namely Jesus, crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.
verses.kjv: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.
verses.bsb: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels, now crowned with glory and honor because He suffered death, so that by the grace of God He might taste death for everyone.

verses.esv: For it was fitting that he, for whom and by whom all things exist, in bringing many sons to glory, should make the founder of their salvation perfect through suffering.
verses.kjv: For it became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
verses.bsb: In bringing many sons to glory, it was fitting for God, for whom and through whom all things exist, to make the author of their salvation perfect through suffering.

verses.esv: For he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified all have one source. That is why he is not ashamed to call them brothers,
verses.kjv: For both he that sanctifieth and they who are sanctified are all of one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,
verses.bsb: For both the One who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are of the same family. So Jesus is not ashamed to call them brothers.

verses.esv: saying, “I will tell of your name to my brothers; in the midst of the congregation I will sing your praise.”
verses.kjv: Saying, I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.
verses.bsb: He says:  “I will proclaim Your name to My brothers; I will sing Your praises in the assembly.”

verses.esv: And again, “I will put my trust in him.” And again, “Behold, I and the children God has given me.”
verses.kjv: And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.
verses.bsb: And again:  “I will put My trust in Him.”   And once again:  “Here am I, and the children God has given Me.”

verses.esv: Since therefore the children share in flesh and blood, he himself likewise partook of the same things, that through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death, that is, the devil,
verses.kjv: Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood, he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil;
verses.bsb: Now since the children have flesh and blood, He too shared in their humanity, so that by His death He might destroy him who holds the power of death, that is, the devil,

verses.esv: and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery.
verses.kjv: And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
verses.bsb: and free those who all their lives were held in slavery by their fear of death.

verses.esv: For surely it is not angels that he helps, but he helps the offspring of Abraham.
verses.kjv: For verily he took not on him the nature of angels; but he took on him the seed of Abraham.
verses.bsb: For surely it is not the angels He helps, but the descendants of Abraham.

verses.esv: Therefore he had to be made like his brothers in every respect, so that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in the service of God, to make propitiation for the sins of the people.
verses.kjv: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.
verses.bsb: For this reason He had to be made like His brothers in every way, so that He might become a merciful and faithful high priest in service to God, in order to make atonement for the sins of the people.

verses.esv: For because he himself has suffered when tempted, he is able to help those who are being tempted.
verses.kjv: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.
verses.bsb: Because He Himself suffered when He was tempted, He is able to help those who are being tempted.

verses.esv: Therefore, holy brothers, you who share in a heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession,
verses.kjv: WHEREFORE, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;
verses.bsb: Therefore, holy brothers, who share in the heavenly calling, set your focus on Jesus, the apostle and high priest whom we confess.

verses.esv: who was faithful to him who appointed him, just as Moses also was faithful in all God's house.
verses.kjv: Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses was faithful in all his house.
verses.bsb: He was faithful to the One who appointed Him, just as Moses was faithful in all God’s house.

verses.esv: For Jesus has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses—as much more glory as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
verses.kjv: For this man was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house.
verses.bsb: For Jesus has been counted worthy of greater glory than Moses, just as the builder of a house has greater honor than the house itself.

verses.esv: (For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.)
verses.kjv: For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.
verses.bsb: And every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.

verses.esv: Now Moses was faithful in all God's house as a servant, to testify to the things that were to be spoken later,
verses.kjv: And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;
verses.bsb: Now Moses was faithful as a servant in all God’s house, testifying to what would be spoken later.

verses.esv: but Christ is faithful over God's house as a son. And we are his house, if indeed we hold fast our confidence and our boasting in our hope.
verses.kjv: But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.
verses.bsb: But Christ is faithful as the Son over God’s house. And we are His house, if we hold firmly to our confidence and the hope of which we boast.

verses.esv: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, “Today, if you hear his voice,
verses.kjv: Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith, To day if ye will hear his voice,
verses.bsb: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says:  “Today, if you hear His voice,

verses.esv: do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, on the day of testing in the wilderness,
verses.kjv: Harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness:
verses.bsb: do not harden your hearts, as you did in the rebellion, in the day of testing in the wilderness,

verses.esv: where your fathers put me to the test and saw my works for forty years.
verses.kjv: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years.
verses.bsb: where your fathers tested and tried Me, and for forty years saw My works.

verses.esv: Therefore I was provoked with that generation, and said, ‘They always go astray in their heart; they have not known my ways.’
verses.kjv: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart; and they have not known my ways.
verses.bsb: Therefore I was angry with that generation, and I said, ‘Their hearts are always going astray, and they have not known My ways.’

verses.esv: As I swore in my wrath, ‘They shall not enter my rest.’”
verses.kjv: So I sware in my wrath, They shall not enter into my rest.)
verses.bsb: So I swore on oath in My anger, ‘They shall never enter My rest.’”

verses.esv: Take care, brothers, lest there be in any of you an evil, unbelieving heart, leading you to fall away from the living God.
verses.kjv: Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.
verses.bsb: See to it, brothers, that none of you has a wicked heart of unbelief that turns away from the living God.

verses.esv: But exhort one another every day, as long as it is called “today,” that none of you may be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
verses.kjv: But exhort one another daily, while it is called To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.
verses.bsb: But exhort one another daily, as long as it is called today, so that none of you may be hardened by sin’s deceitfulness.

verses.esv: For we have come to share in Christ, if indeed we hold our original confidence firm to the end.
verses.kjv: For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end;
verses.bsb: We have come to share in Christ if we hold firmly to the end the assurance we had at first.

verses.esv: As it is said, “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
verses.kjv: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.
verses.bsb: As it has been said:  “Today, if you hear His voice, do not harden your hearts, as you did in the rebellion.”

verses.esv: For who were those who heard and yet rebelled? Was it not all those who left Egypt led by Moses?
verses.kjv: For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.
verses.bsb: For who were the ones who heard and rebelled? Were they not all those Moses led out of Egypt?

verses.esv: And with whom was he provoked for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
verses.kjv: But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?

verses.esv: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, but to those who were disobedient?
verses.kjv: And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?
verses.bsb: And to whom did He swear that they would never enter His rest? Was it not to those who disobeyed?

verses.esv: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.
verses.kjv: So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.
verses.bsb: So we see that it was because of their unbelief that they were unable to enter.

verses.esv: Therefore, while the promise of entering his rest still stands, let us fear lest any of you should seem to have failed to reach it.
verses.kjv: LET us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left us of entering into his rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.
verses.bsb: Therefore, while the promise of entering His rest still stands, let us be careful that none of you be deemed to have fallen short of it.

verses.esv: For good news came to us just as to them, but the message they heard did not benefit them, because they were not united by faith with those who listened.
verses.kjv: For unto us was the gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in them that heard it.
verses.bsb: For we also received the good news just as they did; but the message they heard was of no value to them, since they did not share the faith of those who comprehended it.

verses.esv: For we who have believed enter that rest, as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, ‘They shall not enter my rest,’” although his works were finished from the foundation of the world.
verses.kjv: For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.
verses.bsb: Now we who have believed enter that rest. As for the others, it is just as God has said:  “So I swore on oath in My anger, ‘They shall never enter My rest.’”   And yet His works have been finished since the foundation of the world.

verses.esv: For he has somewhere spoken of the seventh day in this way: “And God rested on the seventh day from all his works.”
verses.kjv: For he spake in a certain place of the seventh day on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.
verses.bsb: For somewhere He has spoken about the seventh day in this manner: “And on the seventh day God rested from all His works.”

verses.esv: And again in this passage he said, “They shall not enter my rest.”
verses.kjv: And in this place again, If they shall enter into my rest.
verses.bsb: And again, as He says in the passage above: “They shall never enter My rest.”

verses.esv: Since therefore it remains for some to enter it, and those who formerly received the good news failed to enter because of disobedience,
verses.kjv: Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief:
verses.bsb: Since, then, it remains for some to enter His rest, and since those who formerly heard the good news did not enter because of their disobedience,

verses.esv: again he appoints a certain day, “Today,” saying through David so long afterward, in the words already quoted, “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts.”
verses.kjv: Again, he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
verses.bsb: God again designated a certain day as “Today,” when a long time later He spoke through David as was just stated: “Today, if you hear His voice, do not harden your hearts.”

verses.esv: For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not have spoken of another day later on.
verses.kjv: For if Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.
verses.bsb: For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not have spoken later about another day.

verses.esv: So then, there remains a Sabbath rest for the people of God,
verses.kjv: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.
verses.bsb: There remains, then, a Sabbath rest for the people of God.

verses.esv: for whoever has entered God's rest has also rested from his works as God did from his.
verses.kjv: For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.
verses.bsb: For whoever enters God’s rest also rests from his own work, just as God did from His.

verses.esv: Let us therefore strive to enter that rest, so that no one may fall by the same sort of disobedience.
verses.kjv: Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of unbelief.
verses.bsb: Let us, therefore, make every effort to enter that rest, so that no one will fall by following the same pattern of disobedience.

verses.esv: For the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and of spirit, of joints and of marrow, and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
verses.kjv: For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.
verses.bsb: For the word of God is living and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it pierces even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow. It judges the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

verses.esv: And no creature is hidden from his sight, but all are naked and exposed to the eyes of him to whom we must give account.
verses.kjv: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
verses.bsb: Nothing in all creation is hidden from God’s sight; everything is uncovered and exposed before the eyes of Him to whom we must give account.

verses.esv: Since then we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold fast our confession.
verses.kjv: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.
verses.bsb: Therefore, since we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold firmly to what we profess.

verses.esv: For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but one who in every respect has been tempted as we are, yet without sin.
verses.kjv: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.
verses.bsb: For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but we have one who was tempted in every way that we are, yet was without sin.

verses.esv: Let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
verses.kjv: Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.
verses.bsb: Let us then approach the throne of grace with confidence, so that we may receive mercy and find grace to help us in our time of need.

verses.esv: For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in relation to God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
verses.kjv: FOR every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins:
verses.bsb: Every high priest is appointed from among men to represent them in matters relating to God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.

verses.esv: He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is beset with weakness.
verses.kjv: Who can have compassion on the ignorant, and on them that are out of the way; for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.
verses.bsb: He is able to deal gently with those who are ignorant and misguided, since he himself is beset by weakness.

verses.esv: Because of this he is obligated to offer sacrifice for his own sins just as he does for those of the people.
verses.kjv: And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.
verses.bsb: That is why he is obligated to offer sacrifices for his own sins, as well as for the sins of the people.

verses.esv: And no one takes this honor for himself, but only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
verses.kjv: And no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as was Aaron.
verses.bsb: No one takes this honor upon himself; he must be called by God, just as Aaron was.

verses.esv: So also Christ did not exalt himself to be made a high priest, but was appointed by him who said to him, “You are my Son, today I have begotten you”;
verses.kjv: So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my Son, to day have I begotten thee.
verses.bsb: So also Christ did not take upon Himself the glory of becoming a high priest, but He was called by the One who said to Him:  “You are My Son; today I have become Your Father.”

verses.esv: as he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
verses.kjv: As he saith also in another place, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
verses.bsb: And in another passage God says:  “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”

verses.esv: In the days of his flesh, Jesus offered up prayers and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to him who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverence.
verses.kjv: Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up prayers and supplications with strong crying and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard in that he feared;
verses.bsb: During the days of Jesus’ earthly life, He offered up prayers and petitions with loud cries and tears to the One who could save Him from death, and He was heard because of His reverence.

verses.esv: Although he was a son, he learned obedience through what he suffered.
verses.kjv: Though he were a Son, yet learned he obedience by the things which he suffered;
verses.bsb: Although He was a Son, He learned obedience from what He suffered.

verses.esv: And being made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him,
verses.kjv: And being made perfect, he became the author of eternal salvation unto all them that obey him;
verses.bsb: And having been made perfect, He became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey Him

verses.esv: being designated by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
verses.kjv: Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
verses.bsb: and was designated by God as high priest in the order of Melchizedek.

verses.esv: About this we have much to say, and it is hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
verses.kjv: Of whom we have many things to say, and hard to be uttered, seeing ye are dull of hearing.
verses.bsb: We have much to say about this, but it is hard to explain, because you are dull of hearing.

verses.esv: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic principles of the oracles of God. You need milk, not solid food,
verses.kjv: For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which be the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.
verses.bsb: Although by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to reteach you the basic principles of God’s word. You need milk, not solid food!

verses.esv: for everyone who lives on milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness, since he is a child.
verses.kjv: For every one that useth milk is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
verses.bsb: For everyone who lives on milk is still an infant, inexperienced in the message of righteousness.

verses.esv: But solid food is for the mature, for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil.
verses.kjv: But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, even those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.
verses.bsb: But solid food is for the mature, who by constant use have trained their senses to distinguish good from evil.

verses.esv: Therefore let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
verses.kjv: THEREFORE leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,
verses.bsb: Therefore let us leave the elementary teachings about Christ and go on to maturity, not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith in God,

verses.esv: and of instruction about washings, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
verses.kjv: Of the doctrine of baptisms, and of laying on of hands, and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.
verses.bsb: instruction about baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.

verses.esv: And this we will do if God permits.
verses.kjv: And this will we do, if God permit.
verses.bsb: And this we will do, if God permits.

verses.esv: For it is impossible, in the case of those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, and have shared in the Holy Spirit,
verses.kjv: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,
verses.bsb: It is impossible for those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, who have shared in the Holy Spirit,

verses.esv: and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come,
verses.kjv: And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,
verses.bsb: who have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the coming age—

verses.esv: and then have fallen away, to restore them again to repentance, since they are crucifying once again the Son of God to their own harm and holding him up to contempt.
verses.kjv: If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open shame.
verses.bsb: and then have fallen away—to be restored to repentance, because they themselves are crucifying the Son of God all over again and subjecting Him to open shame.

verses.esv: For land that has drunk the rain that often falls on it, and produces a crop useful to those for whose sake it is cultivated, receives a blessing from God.
verses.kjv: For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:
verses.bsb: For land that drinks in the rain often falling on it and that produces a crop useful to those for whom it is tended receives the blessing of God.

verses.esv: But if it bears thorns and thistles, it is worthless and near to being cursed, and its end is to be burned.
verses.kjv: But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto cursing; whose end is to be burned.
verses.bsb: But land that produces thorns and thistles is worthless, and its curse is imminent. In the end it will be burned.

verses.esv: Though we speak in this way, yet in your case, beloved, we feel sure of better things—things that belong to salvation.
verses.kjv: But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.
verses.bsb: Even though we speak like this, beloved, we are convinced of better things in your case—things that accompany salvation.

verses.esv: For God is not unjust so as to overlook your work and the love that you have shown for his name in serving the saints, as you still do.
verses.kjv: For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.
verses.bsb: For God is not unjust. He will not forget your work and the love you have shown for His name as you have ministered to the saints and continue to do so.

verses.esv: And we desire each one of you to show the same earnestness to have the full assurance of hope until the end,
verses.kjv: And we desire that every one of you do shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:
verses.bsb: We want each of you to show this same diligence to the very end, in order to make your hope sure.

verses.esv: so that you may not be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
verses.kjv: That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
verses.bsb: Then you will not be sluggish, but will imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.

verses.esv: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
verses.kjv: For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could swear by no greater, he sware by himself,
verses.bsb: When God made His promise to Abraham, since He had no one greater to swear by, He swore by Himself,

verses.esv: saying, “Surely I will bless you and multiply you.”
verses.kjv: Saying, Surely blessing I will bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.
verses.bsb: saying, “I will surely bless you and multiply your descendants.”

verses.esv: And thus Abraham, having patiently waited, obtained the promise.
verses.kjv: And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
verses.bsb: And so Abraham, after waiting patiently, obtained the promise.

verses.esv: For people swear by something greater than themselves, and in all their disputes an oath is final for confirmation.
verses.kjv: For men verily swear by the greater: and an oath for confirmation is to them an end of all strife.
verses.bsb: Men swear by someone greater than themselves, and their oath serves as a confirmation to end all argument.

verses.esv: So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it with an oath,
verses.kjv: Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his counsel, confirmed it by an oath:
verses.bsb: So when God wanted to make the unchanging nature of His purpose very clear to the heirs of the promise, He guaranteed it with an oath.

verses.esv: so that by two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge might have strong encouragement to hold fast to the hope set before us.
verses.kjv: That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us:
verses.bsb: Thus by two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled to take hold of the hope set before us may be strongly encouraged.

verses.esv: We have this as a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul, a hope that enters into the inner place behind the curtain,
verses.kjv: Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and stedfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;
verses.bsb: We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, firm and secure. It enters the inner sanctuary behind the curtain,

verses.esv: where Jesus has gone as a forerunner on our behalf, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.
verses.kjv: Whither the forerunner is for us entered, even Jesus, made an high priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
verses.bsb: where Jesus our forerunner has entered on our behalf. He has become a high priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.

verses.esv: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
verses.kjv: FOR this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;
verses.bsb: This Melchizedek was king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,

verses.esv: and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first, by translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then he is also king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
verses.kjv: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;
verses.bsb: and Abraham apportioned to him a tenth of everything. First, his name means “king of righteousness.” Then also, “king of Salem” means “king of peace.”

verses.esv: He is without father or mother or genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God he continues a priest forever.
verses.kjv: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.
verses.bsb: Without father or mother or genealogy, without beginning of days or end of life, like the Son of God, he remains a priest for all time.

verses.esv: See how great this man was to whom Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth of the spoils!
verses.kjv: Now consider how great this man was, unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.
verses.bsb: Consider how great Melchizedek was: Even the patriarch Abraham gave him a tenth of the plunder.

verses.esv: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to take tithes from the people, that is, from their brothers, though these also are descended from Abraham.
verses.kjv: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:
verses.bsb: Now the law commands the sons of Levi who become priests to collect a tenth from the people—that is, from their brothers—though they too are descended from Abraham.

verses.esv: But this man who does not have his descent from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
verses.kjv: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
verses.bsb: But Melchizedek, who did not trace his descent from Levi, collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.

verses.esv: It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
verses.kjv: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
verses.bsb: And indisputably, the lesser is blessed by the greater.

verses.esv: In the one case tithes are received by mortal men, but in the other case, by one of whom it is testified that he lives.
verses.kjv: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
verses.bsb: In the case of the Levites, mortal men collect the tenth; but in the case of Melchizedek, it is affirmed that he lives on.

verses.esv: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
verses.kjv: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.
verses.bsb: And so to speak, Levi, who collects the tenth, paid the tenth through Abraham.

verses.esv: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
verses.kjv: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.
verses.bsb: For when Melchizedek met Abraham, Levi was still in the loin of his ancestor.

verses.esv: Now if perfection had been attainable through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the law), what further need would there have been for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, rather than one named after the order of Aaron?
verses.kjv: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need was there that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
verses.bsb: Now if perfection could have been attained through the Levitical priesthood (for on this basis the people received the law), why was there still need for another priest to appear—one in the order of Melchizedek and not in the order of Aaron?

verses.esv: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
verses.kjv: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.
verses.bsb: For when the priesthood is changed, the law must be changed as well.

verses.esv: For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
verses.kjv: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.
verses.bsb: He of whom these things are said belonged to a different tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.

verses.esv: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, and in connection with that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
verses.kjv: For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
verses.bsb: For it is clear that our Lord descended from Judah, a tribe as to which Moses said nothing about priests.

verses.esv: This becomes even more evident when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek,
verses.kjv: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
verses.bsb: And this point is even more clear if another priest like Melchizedek appears,

verses.esv: who has become a priest, not on the basis of a legal requirement concerning bodily descent, but by the power of an indestructible life.
verses.kjv: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.
verses.bsb: one who has become a priest not by a law of succession, but by the power of an indestructible life.

verses.esv: For it is witnessed of him, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
verses.kjv: For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
verses.bsb: For it is testified:  “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”

verses.esv: For on the one hand, a former commandment is set aside because of its weakness and uselessness
verses.kjv: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
verses.bsb: So the former commandment is set aside because it was weak and useless

verses.esv: (for the law made nothing perfect); but on the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
verses.kjv: For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope did; by the which we draw nigh unto God.
verses.bsb: (for the law made nothing perfect), and a better hope is introduced, by which we draw near to God.

verses.esv: And it was not without an oath. For those who formerly became priests were made such without an oath,
verses.kjv: And inasmuch as not without an oath he was made priest:
verses.bsb: And none of this happened without an oath. For others became priests without an oath,

verses.esv: but this one was made a priest with an oath by the one who said to him: “The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind, ‘You are a priest forever.’”
verses.kjv: (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)
verses.bsb: but Jesus became a priest with an oath by the One who said to Him:  “The Lord has sworn and will not change His mind: ‘You are a priest forever.’”

verses.esv: This makes Jesus the guarantor of a better covenant.
verses.kjv: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.
verses.bsb: Because of this oath, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.

verses.esv: The former priests were many in number, because they were prevented by death from continuing in office,
verses.kjv: And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:
verses.bsb: Now there have been many other priests, since death prevented them from continuing in office.

verses.esv: but he holds his priesthood permanently, because he continues forever.
verses.kjv: But this man, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood.
verses.bsb: But because Jesus lives forever, He has a permanent priesthood.

verses.esv: Consequently, he is able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, since he always lives to make intercession for them.
verses.kjv: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.
verses.bsb: Therefore He is able to save completely those who draw near to God through Him, since He always lives to intercede for them.

verses.esv: For it was indeed fitting that we should have such a high priest, holy, innocent, unstained, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.
verses.kjv: For such an high priest became us, who is holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;
verses.bsb: Such a high priest truly befits us—One who is holy, innocent, undefiled, set apart from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.

verses.esv: He has no need, like those high priests, to offer sacrifices daily, first for his own sins and then for those of the people, since he did this once for all when he offered up himself.
verses.kjv: Who needeth not daily, as those high priests, to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people’s: for this he did once, when he offered up himself.
verses.bsb: Unlike the other high priests, He does not need to offer daily sacrifices, first for His own sins and then for the sins of the people; He sacrificed for sin once for all when He offered up Himself.

verses.esv: For the law appoints men in their weakness as high priests, but the word of the oath, which came later than the law, appoints a Son who has been made perfect forever.
verses.kjv: For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath, which was since the law, maketh the Son, who is consecrated for evermore.
verses.bsb: For the law appoints as high priests men who are weak; but the oath, which came after the law, appointed the Son, who has been made perfect forever.

verses.esv: Now the point in what we are saying is this: we have such a high priest, one who is seated at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in heaven,
verses.kjv: NOW of the things which we have spoken this is the sum: We have such an high priest, who is set on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens;
verses.bsb: The point of what we are saying is this: We do have such a high priest, who sat down at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in heaven,

verses.esv: a minister in the holy places, in the true tent that the Lord set up, not man.
verses.kjv: A minister of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, and not man.
verses.bsb: and who ministers in the sanctuary and true tabernacle set up by the Lord, not by man.

verses.esv: For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; thus it is necessary for this priest also to have something to offer.
verses.kjv: For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore it is of necessity that this man have somewhat also to offer.
verses.bsb: And since every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices, it was necessary for this One also to have something to offer.

verses.esv: Now if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, since there are priests who offer gifts according to the law.
verses.kjv: For if he were on earth, he should not be a priest, seeing that there are priests that offer gifts according to the law:
verses.bsb: Now if He were on earth, He would not be a priest, since there are already priests who offer gifts according to the law.

verses.esv: They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. For when Moses was about to erect the tent, he was instructed by God, saying, “See that you make everything according to the pattern that was shown you on the mountain.”
verses.kjv: Who serve unto the example and shadow of heavenly things, as Moses was admonished of God when he was about to make the tabernacle: for, See, saith he, that thou make all things according to the pattern shewed to thee in the mount.
verses.bsb: The place where they serve is a copy and shadow of what is in heaven. This is why Moses was warned when he was about to build the tabernacle: “See to it that you make everything according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.”

verses.esv: But as it is, Christ has obtained a ministry that is as much more excellent than the old as the covenant he mediates is better, since it is enacted on better promises.
verses.kjv: But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the mediator of a better covenant, which was established upon better promises.
verses.bsb: Now, however, Jesus has received a much more excellent ministry, just as the covenant He mediates is better and is founded on better promises.

verses.esv: For if that first covenant had been faultless, there would have been no occasion to look for a second.
verses.kjv: For if that first covenant had been faultless, then should no place have been sought for the second.
verses.bsb: For if that first covenant had been without fault, no place would have been sought for a second.

verses.esv: For he finds fault with them when he says: “Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when I will establish a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah,
verses.kjv: For finding fault with them, he saith, Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah:
verses.bsb: But God found fault with the people and said:  “Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah.

verses.esv: not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt. For they did not continue in my covenant, and so I showed no concern for them, declares the Lord.
verses.kjv: Not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant, and I regarded them not, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: It will not be like the covenant I made with their fathers when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not abide by My covenant, and I disregarded them, declares the Lord.

verses.esv: For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws into their minds, and write them on their hearts, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
verses.kjv: For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my laws into their mind, and write them in their hearts: and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:
verses.bsb: For this is the covenant I will make with the house of Israel after those days, declares the Lord. I will put My laws in their minds and inscribe them on their hearts. And I will be their God, and they will be My people.

verses.esv: And they shall not teach, each one his neighbor and each one his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest.
verses.kjv: And they shall not teach every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for all shall know me, from the least to the greatest.
verses.bsb: No longer will each one teach his neighbor or his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ because they will all know Me, from the least of them to the greatest.

verses.esv: For I will be merciful toward their iniquities, and I will remember their sins no more.”
verses.kjv: For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins and their iniquities will I remember no more.
verses.bsb: For I will forgive their iniquities and will remember their sins no more.”

verses.esv: In speaking of a new covenant, he makes the first one obsolete. And what is becoming obsolete and growing old is ready to vanish away.
verses.kjv: In that he saith, A new covenant, he hath made the first old. Now that which decayeth and waxeth old is ready to vanish away.
verses.bsb: By speaking of a new covenant, He has made the first one obsolete; and what is obsolete and aging will soon disappear.

verses.esv: Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly place of holiness.
verses.kjv: THEN verily the first covenant had also ordinances of divine service, and a worldly sanctuary.
verses.bsb: Now the first covenant had regulations for worship and also an earthly sanctuary.

verses.esv: For a tent was prepared, the first section, in which were the lampstand and the table and the bread of the Presence. It is called the Holy Place.
verses.kjv: For there was a tabernacle made; the first, wherein was the candlestick, and the table, and the shewbread; which is called the sanctuary.
verses.bsb: A tabernacle was prepared. In its first room were the lampstand, the table, and the consecrated bread. This was called the Holy Place.

verses.esv: Behind the second curtain was a second section called the Most Holy Place,
verses.kjv: And after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holiest of all;
verses.bsb: Behind the second curtain was a room called the Most Holy Place,

verses.esv: having the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered on all sides with gold, in which was a golden urn holding the manna, and Aaron's staff that budded, and the tablets of the covenant.
verses.kjv: Which had the golden censer, and the ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein was the golden pot that had manna, and Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant;
verses.bsb: containing the golden altar of incense and the gold-covered ark of the covenant. Inside the ark were the gold jar of manna, Aaron’s staff that had budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.

verses.esv: Above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
verses.kjv: And over it the cherubims of glory shadowing the mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.
verses.bsb: Above the ark were the cherubim of glory, overshadowing the mercy seat. But we cannot discuss these things in detail now.

verses.esv: These preparations having thus been made, the priests go regularly into the first section, performing their ritual duties,
verses.kjv: Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service of God.
verses.bsb: When everything had been prepared in this way, the priests entered regularly into the first room to perform their sacred duties.

verses.esv: but into the second only the high priest goes, and he but once a year, and not without taking blood, which he offers for himself and for the unintentional sins of the people.
verses.kjv: But into the second went the high priest alone once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and for the errors of the people:
verses.bsb: But only the high priest entered the second room, and then only once a year, and never without blood, which he offered for himself and for the sins the people had committed in ignorance.

verses.esv: By this the Holy Spirit indicates that the way into the holy places is not yet opened as long as the first section is still standing
verses.kjv: The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:
verses.bsb: By this arrangement the Holy Spirit was showing that the way into the Most Holy Place had not yet been disclosed as long as the first tabernacle was still standing.

verses.esv: (which is symbolic for the present age). According to this arrangement, gifts and sacrifices are offered that cannot perfect the conscience of the worshiper,
verses.kjv: Which was a figure for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service perfect, as pertaining to the conscience;
verses.bsb: It is an illustration for the present time, because the gifts and sacrifices being offered were unable to cleanse the conscience of the worshiper.

verses.esv: but deal only with food and drink and various washings, regulations for the body imposed until the time of reformation.
verses.kjv: Which stood only in meats and drinks, and divers washings, and carnal ordinances, imposed on them until the time of reformation.
verses.bsb: They consist only in food and drink and special washings—external regulations imposed until the time of reform.

verses.esv: But when Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things that have come, then through the greater and more perfect tent (not made with hands, that is, not of this creation)
verses.kjv: But Christ being come an high priest of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;
verses.bsb: But when Christ came as high priest of the good things that have come, He went through the greater and more perfect tabernacle that is not made by hands and is not a part of this creation.

verses.esv: he entered once for all into the holy places, not by means of the blood of goats and calves but by means of his own blood, thus securing an eternal redemption.
verses.kjv: Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own blood he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption for us.
verses.bsb: He did not enter by the blood of goats and calves, but He entered the Most Holy Place once for all by His own blood, thus securing eternal redemption.

verses.esv: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the sprinkling of defiled persons with the ashes of a heifer, sanctify for the purification of the flesh,
verses.kjv: For if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh:
verses.bsb: For if the blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a heifer sprinkled on those who are ceremonially unclean sanctify them so that their bodies are clean,

verses.esv: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God, purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God.
verses.kjv: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?
verses.bsb: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered Himself unblemished to God, purify our consciences from works of death, so that we may serve the living God!

verses.esv: Therefore he is the mediator of a new covenant, so that those who are called may receive the promised eternal inheritance, since a death has occurred that redeems them from the transgressions committed under the first covenant.
verses.kjv: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first testament, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance.
verses.bsb: Therefore Christ is the mediator of a new covenant, so that those who are called may receive the promised eternal inheritance, now that He has died to redeem them from the transgressions committed under the first covenant.

verses.esv: For where a will is involved, the death of the one who made it must be established.
verses.kjv: For where a testament is, there must also of necessity be the death of the testator.
verses.bsb: In the case of a will, it is necessary to establish the death of the one who made it,

verses.esv: For a will takes effect only at death, since it is not in force as long as the one who made it is alive.
verses.kjv: For a testament is of force after men are dead: otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.
verses.bsb: because a will does not take effect until the one who made it has died; it cannot be executed while he is still alive.

verses.esv: Therefore not even the first covenant was inaugurated without blood.
verses.kjv: Whereupon neither the first testament was dedicated without blood.
verses.bsb: That is why even the first covenant was not put into effect without blood.

verses.esv: For when every commandment of the law had been declared by Moses to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,
verses.kjv: For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book, and all the people,
verses.bsb: For when Moses had proclaimed every commandment of the law to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled the scroll and all the people,

verses.esv: saying, “This is the blood of the covenant that God commanded for you.”
verses.kjv: Saying, This is the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.
verses.bsb: saying, “This is the blood of the covenant, which God has commanded you to keep.”

verses.esv: And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and all the vessels used in worship.
verses.kjv: Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.
verses.bsb: In the same way, he sprinkled with blood the tabernacle and all the vessels used in worship.

verses.esv: Indeed, under the law almost everything is purified with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness of sins.
verses.kjv: And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission.
verses.bsb: According to the law, in fact, nearly everything must be purified with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness.

verses.esv: Thus it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these rites, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
verses.kjv: It was therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
verses.bsb: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

verses.esv: For Christ has entered, not into holy places made with hands, which are copies of the true things, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God on our behalf.
verses.kjv: For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, which are the figures of the true; but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us:
verses.bsb: For Christ did not enter a man-made copy of the true sanctuary, but He entered heaven itself, now to appear on our behalf in the presence of God.

verses.esv: Nor was it to offer himself repeatedly, as the high priest enters the holy places every year with blood not his own,
verses.kjv: Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;
verses.bsb: Nor did He enter heaven to offer Himself again and again, as the high priest enters the Most Holy Place every year with blood that is not his own.

verses.esv: for then he would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But as it is, he has appeared once for all at the end of the ages to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
verses.kjv: For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, Christ would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But now He has appeared once for all at the end of the ages to do away with sin by the sacrifice of Himself.

verses.esv: And just as it is appointed for man to die once, and after that comes judgment,
verses.kjv: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:
verses.bsb: Just as man is appointed to die once, and after that to face judgment,

verses.esv: so Christ, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, not to deal with sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
verses.kjv: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.
verses.bsb: so also Christ was offered once to bear the sins of many; and He will appear a second time, not to bear sin, but to bring salvation to those who eagerly await Him.

verses.esv: For since the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered every year, make perfect those who draw near.
verses.kjv: FOR the law having a shadow of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
verses.bsb: For the law is only a shadow of the good things to come, not the realities themselves. It can never, by the same sacrifices offered year after year, make perfect those who draw near to worship.

verses.esv: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, since the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
verses.kjv: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
verses.bsb: If it could, would not the offerings have ceased? For the worshipers would have been cleansed once for all, and would no longer have felt the guilt of their sins.

verses.esv: But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
verses.kjv: But in those sacrifices there is a remembrance again made of sins every year.
verses.bsb: Instead, those sacrifices are an annual reminder of sins,

verses.esv: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
verses.kjv: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
verses.bsb: because it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.

verses.esv: Consequently, when Christ came into the world, he said, “Sacrifices and offerings you have not desired, but a body have you prepared for me;
verses.kjv: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:
verses.bsb: Therefore, when Christ came into the world, He said:  “Sacrifice and offering You did not desire, but a body You prepared for Me.

verses.esv: in burnt offerings and sin offerings you have taken no pleasure.
verses.kjv: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
verses.bsb: In burnt offerings and sin offerings You took no delight.

verses.esv: Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come to do your will, O God, as it is written of me in the scroll of the book.’”
verses.kjv: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
verses.bsb: Then I said, ‘Here I am, it is written about Me in the scroll: I have come to do Your will, O God.’”

verses.esv: When he said above, “You have neither desired nor taken pleasure in sacrifices and offerings and burnt offerings and sin offerings” (these are offered according to the law),
verses.kjv: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
verses.bsb: In the passage above He says, “Sacrifices and offerings, burnt offerings and sin offerings You did not desire, nor did You delight in them” (although they are offered according to the law).

verses.esv: then he added, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He does away with the first in order to establish the second.
verses.kjv: Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.
verses.bsb: Then He adds, “Here I am, I have come to do Your will.” He takes away the first to establish the second.

verses.esv: And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
verses.kjv: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
verses.bsb: And by that will, we have been sanctified through the sacrifice of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

verses.esv: And every priest stands daily at his service, offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
verses.kjv: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:
verses.bsb: Day after day every priest stands to minister and to offer again and again the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.

verses.esv: But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God,
verses.kjv: But this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;
verses.bsb: But when this Priest had offered for all time one sacrifice for sins, He sat down at the right hand of God.

verses.esv: waiting from that time until his enemies should be made a footstool for his feet.
verses.kjv: From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.
verses.bsb: Since that time, He waits for His enemies to be made a footstool for His feet,

verses.esv: For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.
verses.kjv: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
verses.bsb: because by a single offering He has made perfect for all time those who are being sanctified.

verses.esv: And the Holy Spirit also bears witness to us; for after saying,
verses.kjv: Whereof the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us: for after that he had said before,
verses.bsb: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us about this. First He says:

verses.esv: “This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws on their hearts, and write them on their minds,”
verses.kjv: This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their minds will I write them;
verses.bsb: “This is the covenant I will make with them after those days, declares the Lord. I will put My laws in their hearts and inscribe them on their minds.”

verses.esv: then he adds, “I will remember their sins and their lawless deeds no more.”
verses.kjv: And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
verses.bsb: Then He adds:  “Their sins and lawless acts I will remember no more.”

verses.esv: Where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer any offering for sin.
verses.kjv: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.
verses.bsb: And where these have been forgiven, an offering for sin is no longer needed.

verses.esv: Therefore, brothers, since we have confidence to enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus,
verses.kjv: Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus,
verses.bsb: Therefore, brothers, since we have confidence to enter the Most Holy Place by the blood of Jesus,

verses.esv: by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain, that is, through his flesh,
verses.kjv: By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;
verses.bsb: by the new and living way opened for us through the curtain of His body,

verses.esv: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,
verses.kjv: And having an high priest over the house of God;
verses.bsb: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,

verses.esv: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
verses.kjv: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
verses.bsb: let us draw near with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled to cleanse us from a guilty conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.

verses.esv: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who promised is faithful.
verses.kjv: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
verses.bsb: Let us hold resolutely to the hope we profess, for He who promised is faithful.

verses.esv: And let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works,
verses.kjv: And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:
verses.bsb: And let us consider how to spur one another on to love and good deeds.

verses.esv: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.
verses.kjv: Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
verses.bsb: Let us not neglect meeting together, as some have made a habit, but let us encourage one another, and all the more as you see the Day approaching.

verses.esv: For if we go on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
verses.kjv: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,
verses.bsb: If we deliberately go on sinning after we have received the knowledge of the truth, no further sacrifice for sins remains,

verses.esv: but a fearful expectation of judgment, and a fury of fire that will consume the adversaries.
verses.kjv: But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.
verses.bsb: but only a fearful expectation of judgment and of raging fire that will consume all adversaries.

verses.esv: Anyone who has set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
verses.kjv: He that despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:
verses.bsb: Anyone who rejected the law of Moses died without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
