verses.esv: How much worse punishment, do you think, will be deserved by the one who has trampled underfoot the Son of God, and has profaned the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and has outraged the Spirit of grace?
verses.kjv: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?
verses.bsb: How much more severely do you think one deserves to be punished who has trampled on the Son of God, profaned the blood of the covenant that sanctified him, and insulted the Spirit of grace?

verses.esv: For we know him who said, “Vengeance is mine; I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
verses.kjv: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
verses.bsb: For we know Him who said, “Vengeance is Mine; I will repay,” and again, “The Lord will judge His people.”

verses.esv: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
verses.kjv: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
verses.bsb: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

verses.esv: But recall the former days when, after you were enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings,
verses.kjv: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;
verses.bsb: Remember the early days that you were in the light. In those days, you endured a great conflict in the face of suffering.

verses.esv: sometimes being publicly exposed to reproach and affliction, and sometimes being partners with those so treated.
verses.kjv: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
verses.bsb: Sometimes you were publicly exposed to ridicule and persecution; at other times you were partners with those who were so treated.

verses.esv: For you had compassion on those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the plundering of your property, since you knew that you yourselves had a better possession and an abiding one.
verses.kjv: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.
verses.bsb: You sympathized with those in prison and joyfully accepted the confiscation of your property, knowing that you yourselves had a better and permanent possession.

verses.esv: Therefore do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
verses.kjv: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
verses.bsb: So do not throw away your confidence; it holds a great reward.

verses.esv: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God you may receive what is promised.
verses.kjv: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
verses.bsb: You need to persevere, so that after you have done the will of God, you will receive what He has promised.

verses.esv: For, “Yet a little while, and the coming one will come and will not delay;
verses.kjv: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
verses.bsb: For,  “In just a little while, He who is coming will come and will not delay.

verses.esv: but my righteous one shall live by faith, and if he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”
verses.kjv: Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
verses.bsb: But My righteous one will live by faith; and if he shrinks back, I will take no pleasure in him.”

verses.esv: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who have faith and preserve their souls.
verses.kjv: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.
verses.bsb: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who have faith and preserve their souls.

verses.esv: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
verses.kjv: NOW faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
verses.bsb: Now faith is the assurance of what we hope for and the certainty of what we do not see.

verses.esv: For by it the people of old received their commendation.
verses.kjv: For by it the elders obtained a good report.
verses.bsb: This is why the ancients were commended.

verses.esv: By faith we understand that the universe was created by the word of God, so that what is seen was not made out of things that are visible.
verses.kjv: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.
verses.bsb: By faith we understand that the universe was formed at God’s command, so that what is seen was not made out of what was visible.

verses.esv: By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain, through which he was commended as righteous, God commending him by accepting his gifts. And through his faith, though he died, he still speaks.
verses.kjv: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.
verses.bsb: By faith Abel offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. By faith he was commended as righteous when God gave approval to his gifts. And by faith he still speaks, even though he is dead.

verses.esv: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he should not see death, and he was not found, because God had taken him. Now before he was taken he was commended as having pleased God.
verses.kjv: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.
verses.bsb: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he did not see death: “He could not be found, because God had taken him away.” For before he was taken, he was commended as one who pleased God.

verses.esv: And without faith it is impossible to please him, for whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who seek him.
verses.kjv: But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
verses.bsb: And without faith it is impossible to please God, because anyone who approaches Him must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who earnestly seek Him.

verses.esv: By faith Noah, being warned by God concerning events as yet unseen, in reverent fear constructed an ark for the saving of his household. By this he condemned the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes by faith.
verses.kjv: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.
verses.bsb: By faith Noah, when warned about things not yet seen, in godly fear built an ark to save his family. By faith he condemned the world and became heir of the righteousness that comes by faith.

verses.esv: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance. And he went out, not knowing where he was going.
verses.kjv: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.
verses.bsb: By faith Abraham, when called to go to a place he would later receive as his inheritance, obeyed and went, without knowing where he was going.

verses.esv: By faith he went to live in the land of promise, as in a foreign land, living in tents with Isaac and Jacob, heirs with him of the same promise.
verses.kjv: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as in a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:
verses.bsb: By faith he dwelt in the promised land as a stranger in a foreign country. He lived in tents, as did Isaac and Jacob, who were heirs with him of the same promise.

verses.esv: For he was looking forward to the city that has foundations, whose designer and builder is God.
verses.kjv: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.
verses.bsb: For he was looking forward to the city with foundations, whose architect and builder is God.

verses.esv: By faith Sarah herself received power to conceive, even when she was past the age, since she considered him faithful who had promised.
verses.kjv: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.
verses.bsb: By faith Sarah, even though she was barren and beyond the proper age, was enabled to conceive a child, because she considered Him faithful who had promised.

verses.esv: Therefore from one man, and him as good as dead, were born descendants as many as the stars of heaven and as many as the innumerable grains of sand by the seashore.
verses.kjv: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, so many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.
verses.bsb: And so from one man, and he as good as dead, came descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky and as countless as the sand on the seashore.

verses.esv: These all died in faith, not having received the things promised, but having seen them and greeted them from afar, and having acknowledged that they were strangers and exiles on the earth.
verses.kjv: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of them, and embraced them, and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
verses.bsb: All these people died in faith, without having received the things they were promised. However, they saw them and welcomed them from afar. And they acknowledged that they were strangers and exiles on the earth.

verses.esv: For people who speak thus make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
verses.kjv: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.
verses.bsb: Now those who say such things show that they are seeking a country of their own.

verses.esv: If they had been thinking of that land from which they had gone out, they would have had opportunity to return.
verses.kjv: And truly, if they had been mindful of that country from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.
verses.bsb: If they had been thinking of the country they had left, they would have had opportunity to return.

verses.esv: But as it is, they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared for them a city.
verses.kjv: But now they desire a better country, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.
verses.bsb: Instead, they were longing for a better country, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for He has prepared a city for them.

verses.esv: By faith Abraham, when he was tested, offered up Isaac, and he who had received the promises was in the act of offering up his only son,
verses.kjv: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten son,
verses.bsb: By faith Abraham, when he was tested, offered up Isaac on the altar. He who had received the promises was ready to offer his one and only son,

verses.esv: of whom it was said, “Through Isaac shall your offspring be named.”
verses.kjv: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:
verses.bsb: even though God had said to him, “Through Isaac your offspring will be reckoned.”

verses.esv: He considered that God was able even to raise him from the dead, from which, figuratively speaking, he did receive him back.
verses.kjv: Accounting that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.
verses.bsb: Abraham reasoned that God could raise the dead, and in a sense, he did receive Isaac back from death.

verses.esv: By faith Isaac invoked future blessings on Jacob and Esau.
verses.kjv: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
verses.bsb: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning the future.

verses.esv: By faith Jacob, when dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, bowing in worship over the head of his staff.
verses.kjv: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, leaning upon the top of his staff.
verses.bsb: By faith Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of Joseph’s sons and worshiped as he leaned on the top of his staff.

verses.esv: By faith Joseph, at the end of his life, made mention of the exodus of the Israelites and gave directions concerning his bones.
verses.kjv: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.
verses.bsb: By faith Joseph, when his end was near, spoke about the exodus of the Israelites and gave instructions about his bones.

verses.esv: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that the child was beautiful, and they were not afraid of the king's edict.
verses.kjv: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.
verses.bsb: By faith Moses’ parents hid him for three months after his birth, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were unafraid of the king’s edict.

verses.esv: By faith Moses, when he was grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter,
verses.kjv: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter;
verses.bsb: By faith Moses, when he was grown, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter.

verses.esv: choosing rather to be mistreated with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin.
verses.kjv: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;
verses.bsb: He chose to suffer oppression with God’s people rather than to experience the fleeting enjoyment of sin.

verses.esv: He considered the reproach of Christ greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking to the reward.
verses.kjv: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.
verses.bsb: He valued disgrace for Christ above the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking ahead to his reward.

verses.esv: By faith he left Egypt, not being afraid of the anger of the king, for he endured as seeing him who is invisible.
verses.kjv: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.
verses.bsb: By faith Moses left Egypt, not fearing the king’s anger; he persevered because he saw Him who is invisible.

verses.esv: By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood, so that the Destroyer of the firstborn might not touch them.
verses.kjv: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.
verses.bsb: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, so that the destroyer of the firstborn would not touch Israel’s own firstborn.

verses.esv: By faith the people crossed the Red Sea as on dry land, but the Egyptians, when they attempted to do the same, were drowned.
verses.kjv: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.
verses.bsb: By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as on dry land; but when the Egyptians tried to follow, they were drowned.

verses.esv: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
verses.kjv: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.
verses.bsb: By faith the walls of Jericho fell, after the people had marched around them for seven days.

verses.esv: By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient, because she had given a friendly welcome to the spies.
verses.kjv: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.
verses.bsb: By faith the prostitute Rahab, because she welcomed the spies in peace, did not perish with those who were disobedient.

verses.esv: And what more shall I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, of David and Samuel and the prophets—
verses.kjv: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of Barak, and of Samson, and of Jephthae; of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets:
verses.bsb: And what more shall I say? Time will not allow me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets,

verses.esv: who through faith conquered kingdoms, enforced justice, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
verses.kjv: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
verses.bsb: who through faith conquered kingdoms, administered justice, and gained what was promised; who shut the mouths of lions,

verses.esv: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, were made strong out of weakness, became mighty in war, put foreign armies to flight.
verses.kjv: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.
verses.bsb: quenched the raging fire, and escaped the edge of the sword; who gained strength from weakness, became mighty in battle, and put foreign armies to flight.

verses.esv: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Some were tortured, refusing to accept release, so that they might rise again to a better life.
verses.kjv: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:
verses.bsb: Women received back their dead, raised to life again. Others were tortured and refused their release, so that they might gain a better resurrection.

verses.esv: Others suffered mocking and flogging, and even chains and imprisonment.
verses.kjv: And others had trial of cruel mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:
verses.bsb: Still others endured mocking and flogging, and even chains and imprisonment.

verses.esv: They were stoned, they were sawn in two, they were killed with the sword. They went about in skins of sheep and goats, destitute, afflicted, mistreated—
verses.kjv: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;
verses.bsb: They were stoned, they were sawed in two, they were put to death by the sword. They went around in sheepskins and goatskins, destitute, oppressed, and mistreated.

verses.esv: of whom the world was not worthy—wandering about in deserts and mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
verses.kjv: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
verses.bsb: The world was not worthy of them. They wandered in deserts and mountains, and hid in caves and holes in the ground.

verses.esv: And all these, though commended through their faith, did not receive what was promised,
verses.kjv: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:
verses.bsb: These were all commended for their faith, yet they did not receive what was promised.

verses.esv: since God had provided something better for us, that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
verses.kjv: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.
verses.bsb: God had planned something better for us, so that together with us they would be made perfect.

verses.esv: Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight, and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us,
verses.kjv: WHEREFORE seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,
verses.bsb: Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off every encumbrance and the sin that so easily entangles, and let us run with endurance the race set out for us.

verses.esv: looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.
verses.kjv: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.
verses.bsb: Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy set before Him endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.

verses.esv: Consider him who endured from sinners such hostility against himself, so that you may not grow weary or fainthearted.
verses.kjv: For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.
verses.bsb: Consider Him who endured such hostility from sinners, so that you will not grow weary and lose heart.

verses.esv: In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
verses.kjv: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin.
verses.bsb: In your struggle against sin, you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.

verses.esv: And have you forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as sons? “My son, do not regard lightly the discipline of the Lord, nor be weary when reproved by him.
verses.kjv: And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:
verses.bsb: And you have forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as sons:  “My son, do not take lightly the discipline of the Lord, and do not lose heart when He rebukes you.

verses.esv: For the Lord disciplines the one he loves, and chastises every son whom he receives.”
verses.kjv: For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.
verses.bsb: For the Lord disciplines the one He loves, and He chastises every son He receives.”

verses.esv: It is for discipline that you have to endure. God is treating you as sons. For what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
verses.kjv: If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?
verses.bsb: Endure suffering as discipline; God is treating you as sons. For what son is not disciplined by his father?

verses.esv: If you are left without discipline, in which all have participated, then you are illegitimate children and not sons.
verses.kjv: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.
verses.bsb: If you do not experience discipline like everyone else, then you are illegitimate children and not true sons.

verses.esv: Besides this, we have had earthly fathers who disciplined us and we respected them. Shall we not much more be subject to the Father of spirits and live?
verses.kjv: Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected us, and we gave them reverence: shall we not much rather be in subjection unto the Father of spirits, and live?
verses.bsb: Furthermore, we have all had earthly fathers who disciplined us, and we respected them. Should we not much more submit to the Father of our spirits and live?

verses.esv: For they disciplined us for a short time as it seemed best to them, but he disciplines us for our good, that we may share his holiness.
verses.kjv: For they verily for a few days chastened us after their own pleasure; but he for our profit, that we might be partakers of his holiness.
verses.bsb: Our fathers disciplined us for a short time as they thought best, but God disciplines us for our good, so that we may share in His holiness.

verses.esv: For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
verses.kjv: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the peaceable fruit of righteousness unto them which are exercised thereby.
verses.bsb: No discipline seems enjoyable at the time, but painful. Later on, however, it yields a harvest of righteousness and peace to those who have been trained by it.

verses.esv: Therefore lift your drooping hands and strengthen your weak knees,
verses.kjv: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees;
verses.bsb: Therefore strengthen your limp hands and weak knees.

verses.esv: and make straight paths for your feet, so that what is lame may not be put out of joint but rather be healed.
verses.kjv: And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.
verses.bsb: Make straight paths for your feet, so that the lame may not be disabled, but rather healed.

verses.esv: Strive for peace with everyone, and for the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
verses.kjv: Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:
verses.bsb: Pursue peace with everyone, as well as holiness, without which no one will see the Lord.

verses.esv: See to it that no one fails to obtain the grace of God; that no “root of bitterness” springs up and causes trouble, and by it many become defiled;
verses.kjv: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;
verses.bsb: See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God, and that no root of bitterness springs up to cause trouble and defile many.

verses.esv: that no one is sexually immoral or unholy like Esau, who sold his birthright for a single meal.
verses.kjv: Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.
verses.bsb: See to it that no one is sexually immoral, or is godless like Esau, who for a single meal sold his birthright.

verses.esv: For you know that afterward, when he desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no chance to repent, though he sought it with tears.
verses.kjv: For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.
verses.bsb: For you know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected. He could find no ground for repentance, though he sought the blessing with tears.

verses.esv: For you have not come to what may be touched, a blazing fire and darkness and gloom and a tempest
verses.kjv: For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,
verses.bsb: For you have not come to a mountain that can be touched and that is burning with fire; to darkness, gloom, and storm;

verses.esv: and the sound of a trumpet and a voice whose words made the hearers beg that no further messages be spoken to them.
verses.kjv: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which voice they that heard intreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:
verses.bsb: to a trumpet blast or to a voice that made its hearers beg that no further word be spoken.

verses.esv: For they could not endure the order that was given, “If even a beast touches the mountain, it shall be stoned.”
verses.kjv: (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:
verses.bsb: For they could not bear what was commanded: “If even an animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned.”

verses.esv: Indeed, so terrifying was the sight that Moses said, “I tremble with fear.”
verses.kjv: And so terrible was the sight, that Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)
verses.bsb: The sight was so terrifying that even Moses said, “I am trembling with fear.”

verses.esv: But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable angels in festal gathering,
verses.kjv: But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,
verses.bsb: Instead, you have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem. You have come to myriads of angels

verses.esv: and to the assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, and to God, the judge of all, and to the spirits of the righteous made perfect,
verses.kjv: To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,
verses.bsb: in joyful assembly, to the congregation of the firstborn, enrolled in heaven. You have come to God the Judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous made perfect,

verses.esv: and to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks a better word than the blood of Abel.
verses.kjv: And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, that speaketh better things than that of Abel.
verses.bsb: to Jesus the mediator of a new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks a better word than the blood of Abel.

verses.esv: See that you do not refuse him who is speaking. For if they did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth, much less will we escape if we reject him who warns from heaven.
verses.kjv: See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more shall not we escape, if we turn away from him that speaketh from heaven:
verses.bsb: See to it that you do not refuse Him who speaks. For if the people did not escape when they refused Him who warned them on earth, how much less will we escape if we reject Him who warns us from heaven?

verses.esv: At that time his voice shook the earth, but now he has promised, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth but also the heavens.”
verses.kjv: Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.
verses.bsb: At that time His voice shook the earth, but now He has promised, “Once more I will shake not only the earth, but heaven as well.”

verses.esv: This phrase, “Yet once more,” indicates the removal of things that are shaken—that is, things that have been made—in order that the things that cannot be shaken may remain.
verses.kjv: And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.
verses.bsb: The words “Once more” signify the removal of what can be shaken—that is, created things—so that the unshakable may remain.

verses.esv: Therefore let us be grateful for receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, and thus let us offer to God acceptable worship, with reverence and awe,
verses.kjv: Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:
verses.bsb: Therefore, since we are receiving an unshakable kingdom, let us be filled with gratitude, and so worship God acceptably with reverence and awe.

verses.esv: for our God is a consuming fire.
verses.kjv: For our God is a consuming fire.
verses.bsb: “For our God is a consuming fire.”

verses.esv: Let brotherly love continue.
verses.kjv: LET brotherly love continue.
verses.bsb: Continue in brotherly love.

verses.esv: Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
verses.kjv: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
verses.bsb: Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for by so doing some people have entertained angels without knowing it.

verses.esv: Remember those who are in prison, as though in prison with them, and those who are mistreated, since you also are in the body.
verses.kjv: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; and them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.
verses.bsb: Remember those in prison as if you were bound with them, and those who are mistreated as if you were suffering with them.

verses.esv: Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the marriage bed be undefiled, for God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterous.
verses.kjv: Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.
verses.bsb: Marriage should be honored by all and the marriage bed kept undefiled, for God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.

verses.esv: Keep your life free from love of money, and be content with what you have, for he has said, “I will never leave you nor forsake you.”
verses.kjv: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
verses.bsb: Keep your lives free from the love of money and be content with what you have, for God has said:  “Never will I leave you, never will I forsake you.”

verses.esv: So we can confidently say, “The Lord is my helper; I will not fear; what can man do to me?”
verses.kjv: So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
verses.bsb: So we say with confidence:  “The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?”

verses.esv: Remember your leaders, those who spoke to you the word of God. Consider the outcome of their way of life, and imitate their faith.
verses.kjv: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.
verses.bsb: Remember your leaders who spoke the word of God to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life and imitate their faith.

verses.esv: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
verses.kjv: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
verses.bsb: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.

verses.esv: Do not be led away by diverse and strange teachings, for it is good for the heart to be strengthened by grace, not by foods, which have not benefited those devoted to them.
verses.kjv: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.
verses.bsb: Do not be carried away by all kinds of strange teachings, for it is good for the heart to be strengthened by grace and not by foods of no value to those devoted to them.

verses.esv: We have an altar from which those who serve the tent have no right to eat.
verses.kjv: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: We have an altar from which those who serve at the tabernacle have no right to eat.

verses.esv: For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the holy places by the high priest as a sacrifice for sin are burned outside the camp.
verses.kjv: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.
verses.bsb: Although the high priest brings the blood of animals into the Holy Place as a sacrifice for sin, the bodies are burned outside the camp.

verses.esv: So Jesus also suffered outside the gate in order to sanctify the people through his own blood.
verses.kjv: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.
verses.bsb: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate, to sanctify the people by His own blood.

verses.esv: Therefore let us go to him outside the camp and bear the reproach he endured.
verses.kjv: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.
verses.bsb: Therefore let us go to Him outside the camp, bearing the disgrace He bore.

verses.esv: For here we have no lasting city, but we seek the city that is to come.
verses.kjv: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.
verses.bsb: For here we do not have a permanent city, but we are looking for the city that is to come.

verses.esv: Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God, that is, the fruit of lips that acknowledge his name.
verses.kjv: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lips giving thanks to his name.
verses.bsb: Through Jesus, therefore, let us continually offer to God a sacrifice of praise, the fruit of lips that confess His name.

verses.esv: Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have, for such sacrifices are pleasing to God.
verses.kjv: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
verses.bsb: And do not neglect to do good and to share with others, for with such sacrifices God is pleased.

verses.esv: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls, as those who will have to give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with groaning, for that would be of no advantage to you.
verses.kjv: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.
verses.bsb: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch over your souls as those who must give an account. To this end, allow them to lead with joy and not with grief, for that would be of no advantage to you.

verses.esv: Pray for us, for we are sure that we have a clear conscience, desiring to act honorably in all things.
verses.kjv: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.
verses.bsb: Pray for us; we are convinced that we have a clear conscience and desire to live honorably in every way.

verses.esv: I urge you the more earnestly to do this in order that I may be restored to you the sooner.
verses.kjv: But I beseech you the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
verses.bsb: And I especially urge you to pray that I may be restored to you soon.

verses.esv: Now may the God of peace who brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, the great shepherd of the sheep, by the blood of the eternal covenant,
verses.kjv: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,
verses.bsb: Now may the God of peace, who through the blood of the eternal covenant brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great Shepherd of the sheep,

verses.esv: equip you with everything good that you may do his will, working in us that which is pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.
verses.kjv: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: equip you with every good thing to do His will. And may He accomplish in us what is pleasing in His sight through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.

verses.esv: I appeal to you, brothers, bear with my word of exhortation, for I have written to you briefly.
verses.kjv: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.
verses.bsb: I urge you, brothers, to bear with my word of exhortation, for I have only written to you briefly.

verses.esv: You should know that our brother Timothy has been released, with whom I shall see you if he comes soon.
verses.kjv: Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.
verses.bsb: Be aware that our brother Timothy has been released. If he arrives soon, I will come with him to see you.

verses.esv: Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those who come from Italy send you greetings.
verses.kjv: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.
verses.bsb: Greet all your leaders and all the saints.  Those from Italy send you greetings.

verses.esv: Grace be with all of you.
verses.kjv: Grace be with you all. Amen. ¶ Written to the Hebrews from Italy by Timothy.
verses.bsb: Grace be with all of you.
